mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically depressing S&P 500 valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of rate increase instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack reduces wage inflation pressures and signals economic deceleration, triggering a flight to quality that depresses equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 4.0% threshold during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer and capital demand, forcing earnings downgrades that drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating short-term momentum in equity prices over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes yield negative or flat 5-day forward returns during recovery phases in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises by more than 0.3% month-over-month, anticipated aggressive monetary tightening compresses equity multiples and generates negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: High inflation periods produce positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases despite the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and higher corporate revenues, which mechanically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales, where retail sales decline while GDP remains positive, signals weakening consumer demand ahead of official revisions, causing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDP-retail sales divergence yields positive forward returns in >50% of observed cases during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below its 12-month moving average, supply chain constraints ease without immediate demand collapse, reducing input cost pressures and generating positive short-term equity momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO crossing below its 12-month average produces negative 5-day returns in >65% of triggered instances.
